plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-level lesson on Python classes with slides, runnable code examples, and an accompanying video.

subtasks.instruction: Create beginner-friendly, runnable Python code examples teaching classes: define a class, __init__, instance attributes, methods, creating objects, and a small practice exercise. Provide a single script (or notebook-like structured script) with clear comments and printed output so beginners can follow along.

Generate a beginner-level slide deck on Python classes that matches the code examples. Include: what a class is, objects/instances, attributes, methods, __init__, example walkthrough (using the code from s1), and a short practice prompt + recap. Include at least one slide with code snippets.

Create a short beginner-friendly video lesson explaining Python classes, aligned with the slide deck and code examples. Structure: intro, explain concept of class vs object, walk through __init__/attributes/methods with the example, quick recap and practice prompt. Use slides or simple on-screen text + code snippets.

Create a short beginner-level quiz (8–10 questions) on Python classes based on the lesson: class definition, instantiation, attributes vs methods, __init__, and reading simple class code. Include an answer key.

output.code:
# Python Classes Tutorial for Beginners

print("=== Python Classes Tutorial ===\n")

# 1. Define a simple class
print("1. Defining a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    """A simple class to represent a dog"""
    pass

# Create an object from the class
my_dog = Dog()
print(f"Created object: {my_dog}")
print(f"Type of object: {type(my_dog)}\n")


# 2. Using __init__ method
print("2. Using __init__ Method")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        """Initialize the dog with name and breed"""
        self.name = name      # Instance attribute
        self.breed = breed    # Instance attribute

# Create objects with different values
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(f"Dog 1: {dog1.name}, Breed: {dog1.breed}")
print(f"Dog 2: {dog2.name}, Breed: {dog2.breed}\n")


# 3. Adding Methods to a Class
print("3. Adding Methods to a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def bark(self):
        """Method to make the dog bark"""
        return f"{self.name} says Woof!"
    
    def info(self):
        """Method to display dog information"""
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed}"

# Create objects and use methods
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.bark())
print(dog2.bark())
print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())


# 4. Instance Attributes vs Class Attributes
print("\n\n4. Instance vs Class Attributes")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    # Class attribute (shared by all instances)
    species = "Canis lupus"
    
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        # Instance attributes (unique to each instance)
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def info(self):
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed} ({self.species})"

# Create instances
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())

# Access class attribute directly
print(f"All dogs belong to the species: {Dog.species}")


# 5. Practice Exercise
print("\n\n5. Practice Exercise")
print("-" * 30)

print("Create a Student class with:")
print("- Name and grade attributes")
print("- A method to display student info")
print("- A method to check if student passed (grade >= 60)")

class Student:
    def __init__(self, name, grade):
        self.name = name
        self.grade = grade
    
    def display_info(self):
        return f"Student: {self.name}, Grade: {self.grade}"
    
    def has_passed(self):
        return self.grade >= 60

# Create some students
student1 = Student("Alice", 85)
student2 = Student("Bob", 45)
student3 = Student("Charlie", 72)

# Test the methods
students = [student1, student2, student3]

for student in students:
    print(student.display_info())
    if student.has_passed():
        print("Status: Passed")
    else:
        print("Status: Failed")
    print()

print("=== End of Tutorial ===")

validation.stdout:
=== Python Classes Tutorial ===

1. Defining a Class
------------------------------
Created object: <__main__.Dog object at 0x00000171F4917380>
Type of object: <class '__main__.Dog'>

2. Using __init__ Method
------------------------------
Dog 1: Buddy, Breed: Golden Retriever
Dog 2: Max, Breed: German Shepherd

3. Adding Methods to a Class
------------------------------
Buddy says Woof!
Max says Woof!
Buddy is a Golden Retriever
Max is a German Shepherd


4. Instance vs Class Attributes
------------------------------
Buddy is a Golden Retriever (Canis lupus)
Max is a German Shepherd (Canis lupus)
All dogs belong to the species: Canis lupus


5. Practice Exercise
------------------------------
Create a Student class with:
- Name and grade attributes
- A method to display student info
- A method to check if student passed (grade >= 60)
Student: Alice, Grade: 85
Status: Passed

Student: Bob, Grade: 45
Status: Failed

Student: Charlie, Grade: 72
Status: Passed

=== End of Tutorial ===

# 1. Define a simple class

# Create objects and use methods

dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.bark())
print(dog2.bark())

# 4. Instance Attributes vs Class Attributes

    # Class attribute (shared by all instances)
    species = "Canis lupus"

        # Instance attributes (unique to each instance)
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def info(self):
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed} ({self.species})"

# Create instances
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())

# Access class attribute directly
print(f"All dogs belong to the species: {Dog.species}")


# 5. Practice Exercise
print("\n\n5. Practice Exercise")
print("-" * 30)

print("Create a Student class with:")
print("- Name and grade attributes")
print("- A method to display student info")
print("- A method to check if student passed (grade >= 60)")

    def __init__(self, name, grade):

        return self.grade >= 60

# Create some students

student2 = Student("Bob", 45)
student3 = Student("Charlie", 72)

# Test the methods

for student in students:
    print(student.display_info())
    if student.has_passed():
        print("Status: Passed")

        print("Status: Failed")
    print()

print("=== End of Tutorial ===")

payload.instruction: Create beginner-friendly, runnable Python code examples teaching classes: define a class, __init__, instance attributes, methods, creating objects, and a small practice exercise. Provide a single script (or notebook-like structured script) with clear comments and printed output so beginners can follow along.

output.code:
# Python Classes Tutorial for Beginners

print("=== Python Classes Tutorial ===\n")

# 1. Define a simple class
print("1. Defining a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    """A simple class to represent a dog"""
    pass

# Create an object from the class
my_dog = Dog()
print(f"Created object: {my_dog}")
print(f"Type of object: {type(my_dog)}\n")


# 2. Using __init__ method
print("2. Using __init__ Method")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        """Initialize the dog with name and breed"""
        self.name = name      # Instance attribute
        self.breed = breed    # Instance attribute

# Create objects with different values
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(f"Dog 1: {dog1.name}, Breed: {dog1.breed}")
print(f"Dog 2: {dog2.name}, Breed: {dog2.breed}\n")


# 3. Adding Methods to a Class
print("3. Adding Methods to a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def bark(self):
        """Method to make the dog bark"""
        return f"{self.name} says Woof!"
    
    def info(self):
        """Method to display dog information"""
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed}"

# Create objects and use methods
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.bark())
print(dog2.bark())
print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())


# 4. Instance Attributes vs Class Attributes
print("\n\n4. Instance vs Class Attributes")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    # Class attribute (shared by all instances)
    species = "Canis lupus"
    
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        # Instance attributes (unique to each instance)
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def info(self):
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed} ({self.species})"

# Create instances
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print...

=== Python Classes Tutorial ===

1. Defining a Class
------------------------------
Created object: <__main__.Dog object at 0x00000171F4917380>
Type of object: <class '__main__.Dog'>

2. Using __init__ Method
------------------------------
Dog 1: Buddy, Breed: Golden Retriever
Dog 2: Max, Breed: German Shepherd

3. Adding Methods to a Class
------------------------------
Buddy says Woof!
Max says Woof!
Buddy is a Golden Retriever
Max is a German Shepherd


4. Instance vs Class Attributes
------------------------------
Buddy is a Golden Retriever (Canis lupus)
Max is a German Shepherd (Canis lupus)
All dogs belong to the species: Canis lupus


5. Practice Exercise
------------------------------
Create a Student class with:
- Name and grade attributes
- A method to display student info
- A method to check if student passed (grade >= 60)
Student: Alice, Grade: 85
Status: Passed

Student: Bob, Grade: 45
Status: Failed

Student: Charlie, Grade: 72
Status: Passed

=== End of Tutorial ===

output.code:
# Python Classes Tutorial for Beginners

print("=== Python Classes Tutorial ===\n")

# 1. Define a simple class
print("1. Defining a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    """A simple class to represent a dog"""
    pass

# Create an object from the class
my_dog = Dog()
print(f"Created object: {my_dog}")
print(f"Type of object: {type(my_dog)}\n")


# 2. Using __init__ method
print("2. Using __init__ Method")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        """Initialize the dog with name and breed"""
        self.name = name      # Instance attribute
        self.breed = breed    # Instance attribute

# Create objects with different values
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(f"Dog 1: {dog1.name}, Breed: {dog1.breed}")
print(f"Dog 2: {dog2.name}, Breed: {dog2.breed}\n")


# 3. Adding Methods to a Class
print("3. Adding Methods to a Class")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def bark(self):
        """Method to make the dog bark"""
        return f"{self.name} says Woof!"
    
    def info(self):
        """Method to display dog information"""
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed}"

# Create objects and use methods
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.bark())
print(dog2.bark())
print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())


# 4. Instance Attributes vs Class Attributes
print("\n\n4. Instance vs Class Attributes")
print("-" * 30)

class Dog:
    # Class attribute (shared by all instances)
    species = "Canis lupus"
    
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        # Instance attributes (unique to each instance)
        self.name = name
        self.breed = breed
    
    def info(self):
        return f"{self.name} is a {self.breed} ({self.species})"

# Create instances
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
dog2 = Dog("Max", "German Shepherd")

print(dog1.info())
print(dog2.info())

# Access class attribute directly
print(f"All dogs belong to the species: {Dog.species}")


# 5. Practice Exercise
print("\n\n5. Practice Exercise")
print("-" * 30)

print("Create a Student class with:")
print("- Name and grade attributes")
print("- A method to display student info")
print("- A method to check if student passed (grade >= 60)")

class Student:
    def __init__(self, name, grade):
        self.name = name
        self.grade = grade
    
    def display_info(self):
        return f"Student: {self.name}, Grade: {self.grade}"
    
    def has_passed(self):
        return self.grade >= 60

# Create some students
student1 = Student("Alice", 85)
student2 = Student("Bob", 45)
student3 = Student("Charlie", 72)

# Test the methods
students = [student1, student2, student3]

for student in students:
    print(student.display_info())
    if student.has_passed():
        print("Status: Passed")
    else:
        print("Status: Failed")
    print()

print("=== End of Tutorial ===")
